players.injury_note: McBride will undergo surgery for a core muscle injury and may be sidelined until the playoffs, James L. Edwards III of The Athletic reports.
Robinson (ankle) is out for Wednesday's game against the Thunder.
Carlson (back) remains out for Wednesday's game against the Knicks.
Mitchell (ankle) is out for Wednesday's game against New York.
The Thunder announced Friday that Sorber has sustained a torn ACL in his right knee during an offseason workout, NBA reporter Marc Stein reports.
Williams (hamstring) is scheduled to be re-evaluated in approximately two weeks, Brandon Rahbar of DailyThunder.com reports.
Tatum (Achilles) has recently been a "full go in 5-on-5 scrimmages with the Celtics," Shams Charania reported Wednesday on ESPN's "NBA Countdown."
Salaun (knee) is out for Wednesday's game against Boston.
Williams (rest) will not play Wednesday against Boston.
Broome underwent a successful partial meniscectomy Saturday to address a lateral meniscus tear in his right knee, per Adam Zagoria of ZagsBlog.com.
Edgecombe (back) will not play Wednesday against the Jazz, Adam Aaronson of PhillyVoice.com reports.
Embiid will be sidelined for the 76ers' next three games due to a right oblique strain, Tony Jones of The Athletic reports.
The NBA suspended George for 25 games Saturday for violating the league's Anti-Drug program.
Oubre (illness) is out for Wednesday's game against Utah, per Adam Aaronson of PhillyVoice.com.
Jackson was diagnosed with a localized PVNS growth in his left knee and will miss the remainder of the season, Chris Haynes of NBA TV reports.
Kessler will undergo left shoulder surgery and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, Tony Jones of The Athletic reports.
Utah will re-evaluate Markkanen in two weeks after Thursday's MRI showed symptomatic hip impingement with associated inflammation and bone bruise, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Nurkic is scheduled to undergo season-ending nose surgery Wednesday, Chris Haynes of NBA TV reports.
Williams has been diagnosed with a torn left ACL and will miss the remainder of the season, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Aldama (knee) is out for Wednesday's game against Portland.
The Grizzlies announced Thursday that Caldwell-Pope underwent surgery to address the misalignment of his right pinky finger and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 campaign.
The Grizzlies announced Sunday that Clarke (calf) will be re-evaluated in two weeks.
Coward (knee) is doubtful for Wednesday's game against Portland.
Edey underwent surgery Tuesday to resolve discomfort and bone stress in his left ankle, and he'll miss the rest of the 2025-26 season.
Gibson (reconditioning) is out for Wednesday's game versus Portland.
Hendricks is questionable for Wednesday's game against Portland with an illness.
Jerome (calf) is doubtful for Wednesday's game against Portland.
Morant is continuing to experience discomfort during his recovery from a UCL sprain in his left elbow, and he'll be reevaluated in two weeks, the Grizzlies announced.
Pippen (toe) is doubtful for Wednesday's game against Portland.
Avdija (back) is questionable for Wednesday's game against the Grizzlies.
Lillard (Achilles) defeated Devin Booker and Kon Knueppel in the final round of Saturday's 2026 State Farm 3-Point Contest, taking home his third title.
Murray (illness) is listed as questionable for Wednesday's game against the Grizzlies.
Sharpe (calf) will be re-evaluated in 4-to-6 weeks, per Sean Highkin of Rip City Radio 620 Portland.
Porter is out for Wednesday's game against the Hawks due to right knee swelling.

The Bucks applied for a Disabled Player Exception for Prince earlier this month, indicating that they anticipate that he's "substantially more likely than not" to be unable to play through June 15, Eric Nehm of The Athletic reports.
Kuminga is questionable for Wednesday's game versus the Bucks with left knee inflammation, per Brad Rowland of the Locked On Podcast Network.
Beal will undergo season-ending surgery on his left hip, ESPN's Shams Charania reports.
Collins (neck) has been ruled out for Wednesday's game against the Pacers, Joey Linn of SI.com reports.
Furphy was diagnosed with a torn right ACL on Monday and will miss the remainder of the season, Michael Scotto of USA Today reports.

Haliburton said Tuesday in his appearance on the "Mind the Game" podcast that he has resumed taking part in 3-on-3 and 4-on-4 work on the court while he continues to recover from right Achilles surgery.
Nembhard (back) is questionable for Wednesday's game against the Clippers.
Nesmith (ankle) is questionable for Wednesday's game against the Clippers.
Siakam (wrist) is listed as questionable for Wednesday's game against the Clippers.
Toppin is probable for Wednesday's game against the Clippers due to a right foot injury.
Zubac (ankle) has been ruled out for Wednesday's game against the Clippers.
